def: s.m. Running, &c. ( = dhāwā, q.v.).

ety: Prk. धावओ; S. धावकः, rt. धाव्
def: s.m. Running, going expeditiously; a loud and expeditious march, a forced march; an expedition, invasion, overrunning (of a country), incursion, inroad; attack, assault; a rushing or crowding together; a stock, store
subs: (-par), To rush (at), to fall (upon), to attack, assault; to surprise (an enemy)
To make a long and expeditious march, to make a forced march; to make an expedition, to make a sudden attack (on, -par), to invade.

def: s.m. = dhāʼo, q.v.

ety: prob. S. धावकः, rt. धाव 'to run'
def: s.m. A Musalmān caste of pālkī bearers; a member of that caste.
s.m. A man; a husband; possessor, master, lord; — the plant Grislea tomentosa.
adj. Running, going quickly, expeditious, swift.
adj. Cleaning, cleansing, washing.
adj. (f. -ā, or -ī), & s.m. White, dazzling white; handsome, beautiful; — the colour white; — the plant Grislea tomentosa (see dhav; dhau); — name of an inferior rāg or mode in music
subs: s.m. The fortnight of the moon's increase
s.m. 'The white or snowy mountain,' name of one of the highest peaks of the snowy range of the Himālayas

def: s.f. A white cow, &c. (see dhaulā).
adj. Made dazzling white, whitened, white.
adj. Running, &c. ( = dhāvak, q.v.).
s.m. Running, going, moving, motion; a runner, a messenger.
s.m. Washing, cleaning, cleansing, purifying.
s.m. = dhāman or dhāmin, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ḍhām & ḍhāmā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be pulled or thrown down, to cause to be razed, or demolished.
s.f. A species of creeping plant, Hedysarum lagopodioides.
s.f. A kind of prickly nightshade.
adj. Running, going quickly.
part. Running off or away; gone, gone away; run to, advanced towards or against.
part. Purified, cleansed, washed, cleaned, clean.
s.m. (Braj) = dhobī, q.v.

ety: dhāw˚ = Prk. धाव(इ) = S. धाव(ति), rt. धाव्
def: v.n. To run, make haste; to range, roam, rove; to trudge; — v.t. To run or rush at, to attack; to run after, to pursue.
v.t. To worship ( = dhānā, q.v.).
s.f. Bellows ( = dhauṅknī).
s.m. (dialec.) The musician or attendant of a dancing-girl or courtezan; an hermaphrodite; a pimp; a wittol.
s.m. A subdivision of the Jāṭ tribe.
s.f. = ḍhibrī, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhibuʼā or ḍhabuʼā, q.v.
intj. The driver's call to an elephant to kneel that he may be mounted.
s.m. A kind of faqīr; — a tribe of Jāṭs ( = ḍahiyā, ḍhe).
s.m. The Indian crane, Ardea sibirica.
s.f. = ḍheṅklī, q.v.
s.f. = ḍheṅklī, q.v.
s.m. Half a paisā, &c. ( = adhelā, q.v.); half (generally); — fifty rupees (in Broker's speech).

ety: see ḍher; and ḍhepā; and cf. also S. लेष्टु
def: s.m. A lump (of clay, chalk, &c.), mass, a clod (of earth), a pellet (of dried clay, to shoot with); the eye-ball
subs: s.f. The fourth day of the light half of the month Bhādon, and the festival held on that day (in which Hindūs pelt one another with lumps of earth or with stones, and even throw them into one another's houses; and if they are abused, they consider the abuse an honour).

def: s.f. = adhelī, q.v.
s.m. A sling.
s.m. One who uses or throws with a sling, a slinger.

r: ḍhem, ḍhīm, ḍhemā, ḍhīmā, ḍhep, ḍhepā
def: s.m. A lump; a clod; dried clay

r: ḍhemā, ḍhīmā, ḍhem, ḍhīm, ḍhep, ḍhepā
def: s.m. A lump; a clod; dried clay
s.f. A cow ( = دهينو dhenu, q.v.).
s.f. corr. of دهينو dhenu, q.v.
s.m. = ḍheṅḍhā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍheṅṛas, q.v.

ety: prob. another form of ḍhoṅḍh or ḍhoṅḍ, q.v.
def: s.m. A large belly; pregnancy.
s.f. = ḍheṅṛī, q.v.

ety: ḍheṅḍ = ḍhoṅḍ, q.v.+rā = ard = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Seed-vessel, pod (of cotton, hemp, &c.; cf. ḍheṅḍhā, ḍheṅṛī).
s.m. (dialec.) = ḍeg, ḍig, or ḍag, q.v.
s.m. Cudgel; walking-stick.

s.m. A machine for pounding with (a beam working on an upright post as a fulcrum, and having a vertical log, like a pestal, attached to one end, which falls on the substance to be pounded, when the pressure applied at the other extremity in order to raise it, is withdrawn); — the curved piece of wood which passes over the head of the roller of an oil-mill, &c., and presses it down; — the wood by which the moving beam and the upright post in a sugar-mill are joined to one another.

r: ḍheṅkā, ḍheṅk, ḍeg, ḍig, ḍag, lit, ḍaggā, ḍhaggā, Ardea sibirica, ḍhek
def: (ḍheṅk = ḍeg = ḍig or ḍag, q.v.+S. aff. अकः), s.m. lit. 'Taking long strides'; a long-legged person or animal, longshanks ( = ḍaggā or ḍhaggā)
s.m. = ḍhīṅkhar, q.v.
s.f. An apparatus for pounding with.

ety: ḍheṅk = ḍheṅkā, q.v.+lī = Prk. अडिआ = S. ल+इका
def: s.f. A machine for drawing water (being a lever supported on a long post, having a bucket suspended to one end, and a weight of earth or stone at the other); a lever, crane, windlass, &c.; — an apparatus with a lever for pounding with ( = ḍheṅkā, ḍheṅkī); — (met.) a somersault; — a mode of joining breadths of cloth, or of cutting out cloth
s.m. A machine for drawing water ( = ḍheṅklī).

r: ḍheṅṛas, ḍheṅṛ, ḍheṅḍ, ḍhoṅḍ, s, sā
ety: ḍheṅṛ or ḍheṅḍ = ḍhoṅḍ, 'pod'+s = sā, 'like,' q.v.
def: s.m. A kind of vegetable, the ochro or okhra, or gobbo, Alelmoschus esculentus (syn. bhinḍī).
s.m. = ḍheṅḍhā, q.v.

ety: dim. of ḍheṅḍā or ḍheṅḍhā, q.v.
def: s.f. Seed-vessel, pod, capsule (of the poppy, the cotton-tree, hemp, gram, &c.), a poppy-head ( = ḍoṛā); — a capsule-shaped ornament for the ear.
s.f. A milch cow; a cow that has lately calved; — the earth; — an offering, a gift, a present (to Brāhmans)
subs: s.f. A name of the river Gomtī.

def: s.f. A milch cow; — an offering, a gift, a present.

ety: prob. S. स्तम्बः; cf. ḍhebuʼa; ḍhibrī; ḍhabbūs; ḍhappū
def: s.m. Lump, clump, mass (of oil-cake, boiled rice, &c. &c.), clod (of earth); arable land; — ḍhepā, adj. (f. -ī), Lumpy, bulky, massive, large, fat, corpulent.

ety: prob. S. स्तम्बः; cf. ḍhebuʼa; ḍhibrī; ḍhabbūs; ḍhappū
def: s.m. Lump, clump, mass (of oil-cake, boiled rice, &c. &c.), clod (of earth); arable land; — ḍhepā, adj. (f. -ī), Lumpy, bulky, massive, large, fat, corpulent.
s.m. A large quantity, a great deal, abundance, (vulg.) heaps, lots
subs: v.n. To remain or lie in a heap or as a heap, to lie dead, be slain ( = ḍher ho-jānā)
(-kā), To make a heap or pile (of), to pile, store, to accumulate, amass; to make a lifeless mass (of), make a corpse (of), to kill
v.n. To become a heap, to lie in ruins, to fall (as a house); to lie dead, be slain.

def: s.m. A crow; — a low caste of Hindūs who work in leather; an individual of that caste.
s.m.f. Heap, &c. ( = ḍher); — gummy excretion of the eyes.
adj. (dialec.) = ḍherā, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = ḍherā, q.v.

s.m. Two pieces of wood joined together in the form of a cross, and used for twisting and making a ball of coarse thread or twine ( = ṭcrā); the sign of addition (+) in Algebra, plus; the cross or mark made by a person who cannot write; — a large fat louse, or bug, &c. (cf. ḍhīṛh and ḍheṅḍhā); — adj. Squinting, squint-eyed ( = ṭerā).
s.m. A crow; — a low caste of Hindūs who work in leather; an individual of that caste.
s.f. = ḍheṅṛī, q.v.
s.f. A small heap, a heap, &c.; a share in a village or estate, subdivision of a paṭṭī, q.v.
s.m. A share-holder in a village or an estate.
prop. ڐهيري ḍherī, q.v.

ety: see daheṛ, and ḍheṛ, and ḍheṛī-kawwā
def: s.m. A species of bird, the nut-cracker, Nucifraga hemispila (found in the pine forests of the Himālayas); — a species of Coracias.(cf. daheṛ).
s.m. = dheṛī-kawwā, q.v.
s.m. A wave, surge; a whim (cf. ṭeʼo).

ety: S. दौहित्रः+कः; or prob. dhī, q.v.+S. पुत्रकः
def: s.m. Daughter's son, grandson.

ety: S. दौहित्री; or dhī, q.v.+S. पुत्रिका
def: s.f. Daughter's daughter, grand-daughter.
s.f. Understanding, wisdom, intellect, mind, intelligence, sense; knowledge, science, art
subs: (dhī+in˚), s.m. An organ of perception (as the mind, the eye, &c.)
s.f. Power of the mind, intellectual faculty (as attention, comprehension, &c.)
dhī-mat, dhī-mant, or dhī-vān, adj. Possessed of understanding, endowed with intelligence, intellectual, intelligent, sagacious, wise, learned, shrewd, sensible.

ety: S. धीदा or दुहिता; Prk. धीदा, धीआ
def: s.f. Daughter, girl, maiden.

ety: prob. S. स्तिया; cf. ḍhīhā; ḍhūhā
def: s.f. Heap, mound; high bank (of a river, &c.); site of a fallen house, a mass of ruins.
s.m. Lump; protuberance.

ety: prob. S. स्तिभि or स्तम्ब+rī = Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. Nut (of a screw), female screw; a socket (also written ḍhebrī).
s.f. (dialec.) = ḍhīṛ, q.v.
s.f. = dhī or dhiyā, q.v.
s.m. The back.
s.m. 1˚ = ḍhanḍhorā; — 2˚ = ḍhanḍhoriyā, qq.v.
s.m. = ḍhanḍhorā, q.v.
intj. = dhik, q.v.
s.m. Side, quarter; — postpn. By the side (of, -ke), near, close (to).
s.m. A large heap or mass, &c. ( = ḍher, q.v.).
s.m. (dialec.), Heap, pile, store, &c. ( = ḍher, q.v.).
s.m. = ḍhūhā or ḍhūʼā (of which it is prob. but another form).

ety: changeable in comp. to dhig, which is the pop. form
def: intj. Fie! shame! out upon! what a pity! pshaw! pish! — s.f. Curse, execration
subs: vulg. dhikār, s.m. Reproach, censure, contempt; anathema, curse, execration
To swear, lay one's oath; to utter a curse
v.t. To reproach, censure, reprimand; to curse, execrate
s.f. Curse, execration; damnation; — adj. Cursed, accursed, damned.

def: s.m. See s.v. dhik.

ety: Prk. सिढिली and सिढिलं; S. शिथिलः, and शिथिलं
def: adj. = ḍhīlā, q.v.; — s.f. Looseness, relaxedness, slackness, laxity; tardiness, dilatoriness, delay; remissness, negligence, laziness, inattention; — caution, foresight
subs: (-ko), To let loose, to loosen, slacken, relax; to prolong, give a longer time
(-meṅ), To be slack, or lax, or remiss (in), to be tardy, to delay, dawdle, dilly-dally.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Loose, slack, relaxed, free, flabby, flaccid; lax, remiss, negligent, inattentive, dilatory, tardy, slow, lazy, indolent; slovenly, careless, clumsy; weak, impotent; — ḍhīlī ḍorī ḍālnā (-ko), To give a loose rein (to), to grant liberty or licence (to), to let alone.
s.f. = ḍhillāʼī, and dhīl, q.v.
s.f. Laxity, slowness, tardiness, procrastination, &c. (see ḍhīl, and ḍhīlā-pan).
s.m. = ḍhelwāṅs, q.v.

ety: ḍhillā = ḍhīlā+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Slowness, laxity, laziness, indolence, &c. (see ḍhīl; and ḍhīlāpan).
adj. & s.m. Lax, slow, lazy, indolent, &c. ( = ḍhīla, q.v.); — a lazy or indolent man, &c.
adj. & s.m. Lax, slow, lazy, indolent, &c. ( = ḍhīla, q.v.); — a lazy or indolent man, &c.
s.m. Slowness, tardiness; gentleness, mildness, &c. (see dhīmāʼī).

adj. (f. -ī), Tardy, slow, lazy, inactive; gentle, mild, soft, low, subdued (as a tone or voice, &c.), faint, dim, dull (as light, colour, &c.); slight (as fever); temperate, moderate; abated, allayed, lulled, calmed
subs: or paṛ-jānā, v.n. To be or become abated, or lulled, &c., to become faint, or low, or dim, &c.
v.t. To soften, to moderate, mitigate, &c.
or dhīme-dhīme, adv. Slowly; gently, softly, faintly, &c. (cf. dhīre-dhīre).

ety: dhīmā, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका
def: s.f. Slowness, tardiness; gentleness, mildness, softness, faintness, &c. (see dhīmā).
adj. See s.v. dhī.
s.m. = dhīvar, q.v.
adj. = dhī-mān, q.v.s.v. dhī.
s.m. A kind of tamarind.
s.f. = ḍimḍimī, q.v.
s.m. = dhīm, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. dhī.
adj. Stout-bodied, strapping, stalwart, robust, strong; — dhīṅg, s.m. A stalwart fellow, &c.; a paramour, gallant ( = dhaggaṛ)
subs: s.f. Might, power, force, violence, oppression; harassing, worrying, teasing; — commotion, tumult, uproar, hurly-burly (syn. hūṛā-hūṛī); — adv. Violently, forcibly, by force
or dheṅgā-mushṭī, s.f. A scuffle, fisticuffs, fight, row

def: = H دهنگا धिंगा dhingā, adj. corr. of dhīng, and dhīngā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of pitchfork or rake (used on the threshing-floor; syn. jelī, q.v.).
adj. Stout-bodied, strapping, stalwart, robust, strong; — dhīṅg, s.m. A stalwart fellow, &c.; a paramour, gallant ( = dhaggaṛ)
subs: s.f. Might, power, force, violence, oppression; harassing, worrying, teasing; — commotion, tumult, uproar, hurly-burly (syn. hūṛā-hūṛī); — adv. Violently, forcibly, by force
or dheṅgā-mushṭī, s.f. A scuffle, fisticuffs, fight, row

def: = H دهنگ धिंग dhing, adj. corr. of dhīng, and dhīngā, q.v.
v.t. To oppress, harass, worry, tease; — s.m. Oppression; teasing; — clamour.

ety: dhīṅg, q.v.+ṛā = Prk. अडओ = S. अ+र+कः
def: s.m. = dhīṅg.

s.m. A kind of harrow, consisting of a bundle of thorns tied together and drawn by bullocks (used for the purpose of beating out grain from the ear; and also for eradicating grass and weeds from ploughed land).
s.m. = dhīvar, q.v.

adj. (f. -ā), Steady, holding firmly (to), steadfast; enduring, patient; durable, lasting, stable, continual, constant; firm, determined, resolute; deliberate, cool, calm, collected, self-possessed; energetic, persevering; courageous, brave, bold; sedate, grave, sober, solemn; deep, hollow, low, bass (as a voice or sound); inactive, sluggish, slow, lazy, dull; soft, gentle, mild (see dhīrā).

s.m. Steadiness, steadfastness, firmness; endurance, patience; durability, stability, strength, consistency, constancy; resoluteness, inflexibleness; fortitude, courage, bravery; deliberateness, coolness, calmness, composure, self-possession; sedateness, gravity, soberness
adj. (f. -ī) = dhīr, q.v.
subs: or dhīre-dhīre, adv. Slowly; deliberately; patiently; gently, softly.

def: s.m. contrac. of adhirāj, q.v.
s.m. = dhīr, q.v.
subs: (or dhīr) bāṅdhnā or bandhānā (-ko), To inspirit, encourage; to cheer, solace, to "set (one's) heart at ease
(or dhīr) dharnā, dhīraj rakhnā, dhīraj karnā, v.n. To exercise patience, have patience or perseverance, to take courage, to be firm; to be composed, be calm, be deliberate, be imperturbed
or dhīraj-wān, dhīraj-wat, dhīraj-want, adj. Endowed with endurance, patient; resolute, firm; deliberate, collected, composed; sedate, &c. (see dhīr)
adj. Steady, calm, assuming composure or firmness.

def: s.f. = H دهيرجتو धीरजत्व dhīrajtwa, s.m. (barbarous forms) = dhīraj, or dhīr, q.v.
s.m. = H دهيرجتا धीरजता dhīrajtā, s.f. (barbarous forms) = dhīraj, or dhīr, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. ध्ट; but cf. dhir-kār
def: v.t. To threaten, to bully.
s.f. = S دهيرتو धीरत्व dhīratva, s.m. = dhīr, q.v.
s.m. = S دهيرتا धीरता dhīratā, s.f. = dhīr, q.v.
s.m. = ḍheṅḍhā, or ḍhīṅṛhā, q.v.
s.m. Name of an inferior caste of Ahīrs in Benares.
s.f. = dhīr, dhīraj, q.v.
s.f. The pupil of the eye.
s.f. Daughter.
s.m. Name of a caste who are bamboo workers.
intj. = dhik-kār, q.v.s.v. dhik.
v.n. To give way, sink, fall in or down, to yield

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Bold, daring, fearless, confident; familiar, forward, presumptuous, overweening; impudent, barefaced, shameless; insolent, rude, saucy, pert; obstinate, stubborn, wilful; — a bold, or impudent man, &c.
s.f. = ḍhiṭhāʼī, q.v.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Bold, daring, fearless, confident; familiar, forward, presumptuous, overweening; impudent, barefaced, shameless; insolent, rude, saucy, pert; obstinate, stubborn, wilful; — a bold, or impudent man, &c.

ety: Prk. दिट्ठो and दिट्ठओ; S. दृष्टः and दृष्ट+कः

adj. = ḍhīt, or ḍhīṭh, q.v.

ety: ḍhīṭh, q.v.+āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. aff. ता+इका

def: s.f. = ḍhiṭhāʼī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = ḍhīṭh, q.v.
adj. See s.v. dhī.
s.m. The caste of fisher; one of that caste ( = kahār), a fisherman, fisher ( = dhīmar).
s.f. A fisherman's wife; — a sort of harpoon for catching fish.

ety: S. धीदा or दुहिता;Prk. धीदा, धीआ
def: s.f. Daughter, girl, maiden.

ety: S. दुहितृणां, gen. plur. of दुहितृ; cf. ghar and gharānā
def: s.m. (f. -ī), Sons-in-law, or brothers-in-law (of a family).

s.f. Daughters, or sisters (of a family).

s.m. corr. of droh, q.v.
s.m. A present of fruit and flowers (from an inferior to a superior) on festival days (cf. ḍālī).
s.m. A present of fruit, &c. ( = ḍhoʼā).

ety: v.n. fr. dhobnā = dhownā ( = dhonā); — or dho(nā)+āb = Prk. एअव्वं = S. (इ)तव्यं
def: s.m. Washing; a wash
subs: v.n. To be washed ( = dhoyā or dhoʼā jānā).

def: s.m. A washerman
subs: s.m. A washerman's board or stone (on which he beats the clothes when washing); — a trick in wrestling (by which a wrestler throws his adversary with both hands as a washerman dashes down his clothes on the stone).

ety: dhob, q.v.+ Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. A washerman's wife; a washerwoman, laundress
subs: s.f. A wagtail (so called from the bird's frequenting the ghāṭs where dhobīs wash clothes; syn. mamolā).

ety: dhob, q.v.+ Prk. इणी = S. इनी
def: s.f. A washerman's wife; a washerwoman, laundress

def: s.m. Hole, breach, fissure (in a vessel, or wall, &c.); an earthen vessel with a hole or holes in it, an old, broken vessel; an old, broken, or dilapidated house, or wall.
s.m. = ḍhoṛā, ḍhoṛhā, q.v.
s.m. = ḍhoṛā, ḍhoṛhā, q.v.
part. (of dhonā-dhānā; see s.v. dhonā), Having thoroughly washed and cleansed.
s.f. A sour belch.
s.f. A sour belch.

s.f. = dhok, q.v.
s.m. A large piece of broken stone; — small stones of an inferior quality extracted from the Chunār quarries.

subs: v.n. To bow down (before an idol), &c. ( = dhoknā, q.v.).

def: s.m. Gulp, draught, large mouthful (syn. ghūṅṭ).
s.m. = dhokhā, q.v.
s.m. A large piece of broken stone; — small stones of an inferior quality extracted from the Chunār quarries.
adj. Large, bulky, robust, burly, lusty, sturdy, stout, strong, athletic.

s.m. Deceit, deception, delusion; blunder, mistake; disappointment, baulking; doubt, hesitation; alarm, panic; anything that may deceive or mislead, false appearance, a scarecrow; anything imaginary or unreal, a mirage; an object or form indistinctly seen at a distance
subs: (-ko), To deceive, cheat, take in; to mislead; to disappoint, baulk
(-se), dhokhe-meṅ ānā (-ke), To be deceived (by), be deluded, be taken in; to be misled; to be mistaken (in respect of); to be disappointed or baulked
(-ko), To keep one in a delusion, to feed with false hopes or promises

def: v.t. To gulp large draughts of, to gulp down, quaff, guzzle, swill, tope.
v.n. To bow down (before an idol), to make a salutation; to lean (against = ḍhoknā).
s.m. A large drum or tabor (cf. P. duhul); — a fixed pulley.

ety: Prk. ढोल्लओ; S. धूर्तकः; cf. ḍhoṭā
def: s.m. Name of a famous lover; a lover, gallant; a gay deceiver; — a darling, pet; boy, child, son.
s.f. = dhulāʼī, q.v.
s.f. = ڐهلائي ḍhulāʼī, q.v.
s.f. A small drum.
s.m.f. A lover, sweetheart; a friend.
s.m. A drummer.
s.f. A heap or bundle of about two hundred pān or betel-leaves.
s.m. A drummer.
s.f. A small drum.
One who plays on a small drum, a drummer.
One who plays on a small drum, a drummer.

ety: ḍhol, q.v.+nā = lā = Prk. अडओ = S. ल+कः
def: s.m. A small amulet in the shape of a drum; a miniature copy of the Qorʼān, &c. worn as an amulet.

ety: dho˚ = Prk. धोअ(इ) = S. धाव(ति), rt. धाव् (or धुव(ति), rt. धू
def: v.t. To wash, cleanse
subs: v.t. To wash and cleanse thoroughly
v.t. (intens.) To wash out, rinse out, clean.

def: v.t. To take or bear up (a load), to carry, transport, remove.
adj. & s.m. See ḍhoṅćā.

ety: Ap. Prk. अड्ढौंचउ, for अड्ढवंचउ = S. अर्ध+पञ्चम+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Four and a half; — the sum of four times a number or quantity added to its half; the pahāṛā or multiplication table of 4½ times (also written dhoṅćā).

ety: S. तुण्ड, or तुन्द, or स्थूल+तुन्दं; cf. ṭhuḍḍī, and ḍoḍā or ḍorā
def: s.m. Seed-vessel, pod, capsule (of poppy, cotton, gram, pulse, &c.); — 2˚ = ḍhoṅḍī or ḍhoṅṛī, q.v.
s.m. A kind of grass which grows in rice fields (and sometimes chokes the plant).
s.m. (dialec.) Stone, rock.

ety: S. तुण्ड, or तुन्द, or स्थूल+तुन्दं; cf. ṭhuḍḍī, and ḍoḍā or ḍorā
def: s.m. Seed-vessel, pod, capsule (of poppy, cotton, gram, pulse, &c.); — 2˚ = ḍhoṅḍī or ḍhoṅṛī, q.v.
adj. Abounding in stones or rocks, stony, rocky.
s.m. Seed-vessel, pod, &c. ( = ḍhoṅḍ, q.v.).
s.m. A small mound (of earth), a small hillock (syn. ṭīlā); — (fig.) a large belly, a pot-belly.
s.f. A kind of grass ( = dhoṅḍā, q.v.).
s.f. (dialec.) = ḍoṅḍī, q.v.
s.m. Deceit, fraud, cheating, imposture, trickery, artifice, trick, feigning, hypocrisy, sanctimoniousness, pretence, sham, arrogant or fraudulent assumption or display
subs: (-kā), To make a pretence (of), to make a false show, to practise deception, to impose (upon).

def: s.m. Rump, buttock; hip; side; cut, slit, or opening in the side (of a gown, shirt, &c.).

ety: ḍhoṅg, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Deceitful, fraudulent, hypocritical; deceptive, delusive; a deceitful man, a knave, impostor, pretender, hypocrite.

ety: ḍhoṅg, q.v.+ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः
def: adj. & s.m. Deceitful, fraudulent, hypocritical; deceptive, delusive; a deceitful man, a knave, impostor, pretender, hypocrite.
s.m. Gulp, &c. ( = ḍhok, q.v.)
v.t. = ḍhoknā, q.v.
s.f. = ḍhoṅḍī, q.v.
s.f. = ḍhos, q.v.
s.f. A shepherd's crook; — an instrument for digging up with or cutting down.

ety: fr. S. धाव् 'to run'; cf. dhob
def: s.f. Running, a race; exertion.
s.f. A kind of sword (long and straight); a blow or stroke with the flat of this sword, or with a cudgel; (fig.) a broad leaf or blade.
s.m. A species of water-snake.
s.m. Cattle, beast, brute (generally)
s.m. A kind of medicine (cf. dhatūrā).
s.m. = ḍhoṛā, q.v.
s.m. A kind of weevil (very destructive to chick-pea or 'gram').
s.m. Heap or mound of earth (raised by the side of a ditch).
s.m. The representation of the tomb of Ḥasan and Ḥusain carried about in the Moḥarram (syn. tāziya; dahā); — a house; a shop; an empty show
subs: (-kā), To keep up the appearance of state or dignity (after it is lost).

def: s.f. An uninterrupted series; tradition.
adv. Closely, approximately.
s.m. = ḍhoṛā, q.v.
s.m. = dhūrta, dhūrt, q.v.
s.m. An ox, bullock; cattle ( = ḍhor).
s.f. Eagerness, ardour.
s.f. = adhauṛī, q.v.

ety: prob. fr. S. ध्वंस्; cf. ṭhos-nā
def: s.m. Thrust, stab.
s.m. A coarse kind of shawl (cf. dhussā).
intj. = dhut; dut, qq.v.
adj. & s.m. Cunning, crafty, dishonest, false, treacherous, perfidious; — a cheat, knave, rogue.
s.m. Child, son (esp. a fine, large, robust child).
s.f. (dialec.) = dhotī, q.v.
s.f. A kind of cloth (manufactured in India).
s.f. A cloth worn round the waist, passing between the legs and tucked in behind
subs: dhotī-prasād, s.m. One who wears a dhotī; (contemp.) a Hindū (esp. a Brāhman, or a Baniyā).

def: s.m. A kind of falcon (the female of which is called besarā).
s.f. (dialec.) = dhotī, q.v.
part. adj. = dhoyā or dhoʼā, q.v.
s.m. Water in which anything (as clothes, dishes, rice, &c.) has been washed; a washed garment, &c. (also written dhoʼan, dhoṅʼan).

ety: dho(nā)+-wat = āwat = Prk. आवती = S.आप्ति (i.e. आपआ caus. augment+aff. ति)
def: s.f. Washing.
v.t. (dialec.) = dhonā, q.v.
part. adj. Washed, &c. (also written dhoʼā; see dhoʼī)
intj. = dhik, q.v.
adj. Bold, daring, confident; insolent, impudent, rude, shameless; forward, presumptuous ( = ḍhīṭh).
s.f. = S دهرشٿتو धृष्टत्व dhr̤ishṭatva, s.m. Boldness, confidence; impudence, insolence; forwardness, &c.
s.m. = S دهرشٿتا धृष्टता dhr̤ishtatā, s.f. Boldness, confidence; impudence, insolence; forwardness, &c.
part. Held, borne, carried; maintained, supported, sustained; contained; possessed, kept, retained, detained; laid hold of, seized, grasped
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. Firm-minded, self-possessed, steady, calm, collected
s.m. 'Whose empire is firm, or who holds the kingdom'; name of a king, father of Duryodan, and uncle of the five Pāṇḍu princes.

def: s.f. Holding, holding fast, having, possessing, keeping; firmness, constancy, steadiness, steadfastness, fortitude, energy, resolution; strong will, self-command; satisfaction, contentment, happiness; — a species of metre (in which the stanza consists of four lines of eighteen syllables each)
subs: dhr̤iti-mant, dhr̤iti-mān, adj. Steadfast, firm, steady, calm, determined, resolute; satisfied, content.

def: s.m. = dhruv, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = dhruv, q.v.

adj. Fixed, firm, immovable, stable, enduring, constant, lasting, permament; certain, sure, true, right; — s.m. The polar star, the north pole (personified in mythology as son of Uttāna-pāda and grandson of the first Manu); name of one of the twenty-seven astronomical Yogas; a name of Vishnu, and of others; — permanence, fixedness, &c.
subs: s.m. (in Myth.) One of the divisions of paradise.

def: s.f. A virtuous woman; — the plant Desmodium gangeticum or Hedysarum gangeticum; the tree Sanseviera zeylanica (from the fibres of which bow-strings are made); — the introductory stanza of a song, &c. ( = dhur-pad, q.v.s.v. dhur).
adj. & s.m. Big, bulky, burly, strapping, sturdy, strong; — a big, burly, cr strapping fellow.
s.m. A heap of mud or earth, a mound, a boundary mark ( = ḍholā).
s.m. = dhūʼāṅ, q.v.

ety: Prk. धूवँउ or धूमओ = S. धूम+कः
def: s.m. Smoke; vapour
subs: To reduce to poverty; also = dhajjiyāṅ uṛānā, q.v.s.v. dhajjī
'To scatter as smoke'; to exhaust (one's) resources; — to render speechless, to silence
s.m. A sharer in smoke and water; next-door or near neighbour

s.m. A cloud of smoke, much or dense smoke; — adj. Smoky; hazy, misty, foggy; cloudy, dark, black, dense (as clouds); bright, beautiful, adorned; — excessive; fuming, boiling, raging; — adv. Excessively, heavily, in torrents (e.g. dhūʼāṅ-dhār barasnā)
s.m. An exit for smoke, a chimney, funnel (of a steam-ship); a steamship ( = dhūʼeṅ-kash-kā jahāz)
s.m. One who smokes at another's expense, a hanger on or sponge.

def: s.m. An exit for smoke, a chimney ( = dhūṅwālā, q.v.).
adj. Smoked, smoky; — s.m. Soot; anything smoky or about which soot collects, smoked cobwebs, &c.
s.m. (prov.) = dhobī, q.v.
s.f. The rump-bone, root of the tail, the coccyx; — an inferior kind of ḥuqqa.
s.f. The rump-bone, root of the tail, the coccyx; — an inferior kind of ḥuqqa.
s.m. A driving away; — intj. Off, off! away! &c. ( = dur-dur; dhut-dhut, qq.v.).
s.f. = dhukdhukī, q.v.
s.f. = dhugdhugī, dhukdhukī, q.v.
s.m. Mound; (local) elevated ground in the midst of ravines.
s.m. = ḍhūʼā, and ḍholā, qq.v.
s.m. A scarecrow.
s.f. Vacillation; putting off
s.m. Crouching; stealing up to; peeping, peep; touching, poking, tapping, shoving, nudging; tap, touch, nudge (by way of calling one's attention to anything); — hiding-place, lair
subs: (-ko), To peep; to touch, tap, nudge, &c.

def: s.f. A purse.
s.f. Beating, palpitation; — agitation, anxiety; suspense, uncertainty; wavering, indecision.
s.f. Beating, palpitation; — agitation, anxiety; suspense, uncertainty; wavering, indecision.

ety: ḍhukk (the Prk. form of ḍhuk˚)+ī = Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. The act of bending, or stooping, or crouching (as of a cat, or a tiger, preparatory to springing on its prey); looking, peeping, peep, taking aim
subs: To take a peep (at, or into), to take aim (at)
To bob or duck; to crouch ready for a spring (as a cat, or tiger).

ety: ḍhuk(nā) + lī = alī = Prk. अडिआ = S. ल or र+इका
def: s.f. A machine for raising water ( = ḍheṅklī, q.v.).

ety: ḍhūk˚ = Prk. ढुक्क(इ) = S. ढौक(ते), rt. ढौक्
def: v.n. To approach, draw near; to steal (upon); to crouch; to lurk; to peep; to go (into), enter; to shut, close, &c. (see ḍhuknā).

v.n. To approach, to come or go (towards); to incline (to or towards), to tend (to or towards); to bend, bend down or forward, to stoop, duck: to crouch (ready for a spring); to take aim (at); to look, peep (at); to return, revert, come back; — to have regard (to); to sympathize; to enter, penetrate, make a way (into).
v.t. = ḍhakosnā, q.v.
s.f. Dust (syn. ḵẖāk)
subs: To raise, or throw, dust; to raise a dust, make a commotion; to disgrace, defame
v.n. Dust to blow or fly about; a commotion to be raised; to be reduced to poverty; to be disgraced
adj. Trampled under foot, ruined; dispersed; troubled, perplexed
s.m. A washer of refuse or dust
lit. 'To twist a rope of sand'; to attempt an impossibility, to labour in vain

ety: S. स्थूल+कं or स्तूप+ल+कः; cf. ḍhūhā
def: s.m. A heap or mound of earth, a pillar of mud or brick, boundary-mark, landmark; bank (of a river, &c.); a scarecrow ( = ḍhūʼā); — a log fastened to the neck of a vicious ox ( = ḍenā)
subs: s.f. Marking a boundary, &c. with mud heaps or pillars.

def: s.f. Washing; price paid for washing
subs: s.f. The (barber's) fee for washing the hands of the bride and bridegroom (before the marriage feast).

def: s.f. The having a thing carried or transported; — the price paid for having a thing carried, &c.
s.m. Poison.
v.n. To roll, roll down, slip, slide; to topple over, fall over or down; to be pushed or forced down
v.t. To cause to be washed, to get (a thing) washed.
v.t. To cause to be carried, to have or get removed, or transported, &c.
v.t. To incline, make inclined, to tilt; to cause to run over, to spill.

r: ḍhulāʼo, ḍhalāʼo, ḍhul, nā, ḍhal, āʼo, āʼū, ḍhalāʼū
def: s.m. Inclination, slope, declivity
subs: s.m. Angle of inclination (of a plane).

def: s.f. The first day of the month Ćait (on which it is the practice of the Hindūs to scatter ashes); the second day of the Holī festival (on which they throw a red powder called abīr or gulāl on one another).
s.f. Dust, powder
subs: s.m. A washer of dust or refuse ( = dhūl-dhoyā)
s.m. The red fragrant vegetable powder thrown about at the Holī festival.

def: adj. & s.m. Of dust, made or composed of dust; — anything made of dust; a god of dust (made by children in play); — s.f. Dust ( = dhūlī or dhūlikā)
subs: v.t. 'To make dust and earth of,' or 'to cover with dust and earth'; to quash or hush up (an affair).

def: v.t. = dhuriyānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to roll, or topple over, or fall, or slip, or slide; to roll, roll down; to tilt, to incline; — to incline (one) to, to incite, instigate.
v.n. To swing; to roll.
